artPrompt: A characterful portrait of a portly goblin crime boss in a pinstripe suit and too-wide hat, leaning back in a worn leather chair behind a card table, one clawed hand extended in a generous-but-dangerous gesture, a fat cigar glowing in the other. A single amber lamp carves his sly grin from the dark, smoke curling upward. Mono-black noir of ink shadow and smoky brown, intimate criminal charm, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Don Guttersnipe, Who Knows a Guy
type: Legendary Acorn Creature — Goblin Crime Boss
text:
When Don Guttersnipe enters the battlefield, do a favor for each opponent: each opponent draws a card. For each card drawn this way, you create a Favor token. (A Favor is a colorless artifact token with "Sacrifice this artifact: Choose a player who owes you a Favor. That player loses 1 life or sacrifices a permanent. You decide which, capisce." The player it's spent on is the one the Don did the favor for.)
The Family Looks After Its Own — Whenever you give an opponent a card, a Treasure, a snack, life, or a vote, you create a Favor token. Nothing's free in this town.
{2}{B}, Sacrifice three Favors: Make target opponent an offer they can't refuse. That player sacrifices a creature and a noncreature permanent of your choice, then says "thank you, Don" out loud.
flavorText: "You needed a favor. Now you owe one. That's just how friendship works in Whateverville."
